The correct answer is D, as it accurately captures the biconditional structure of (A ∨ ~E) ≡ (D • ~B) by using "just in case" to mean "if and only if." Option A incorrectly describes a necessary condition only, and option B changes the negation of B. Option C uses "unless" in a way that can be ambiguous, while D directly matches the logical form.  

\boxed{D}
